notes: AR permission fix for iPhone (2026-06). Symptom: tapping AR on iPhone showed the 'permission required' toast but NO iOS dialog. Cause: DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() was called inside the async _arEnter() — even as its first await, iOS can fail the transient-activation (user-gesture) check there and silently resolve 'denied'. Fix: requestPermission() is now fired SYNCHRONOUSLY in the tap handler (toggleARMode), and the pending promise is handed to _arEnter(); verified the call happens synchronously within the gesture. Also request DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission() (some iOS gate orientation data behind it). On a hard denial the toast now points to the exact iOS setting (motion: Settings>Safari>Motion & Orientation Access; camera: Settings>Safari>Camera) since iOS never re-prompts once blocked. Builds on v0.0.14.
